plan: 1. Create shadow profile card UI component at src/ui/shadow-profile-card.ts — a createShadowProfileCard(inference: InferenceStatement) function returning an HTMLElement (<article class="card shadow-profile-card">) with: prominent [Inference] marker badge, statement text, evidence list (signal name + value + source per entry), and confidence badge (reuse low/medium/high color scheme from osint-card). Follow the pure-function DOM-creation pattern from src/ui/osint-card.ts.

2. Create shadow profile card list component — a createShadowProfileCardList(cards: HTMLElement[]) function returning a grid container div, following the same responsive grid pattern as createOsintCardList (1 col mobile, 2 col at 768px+).

3. Create shadow profile stylesheet at src/modules/shadow-profile/shadow-profile.css — style the .shadow-profile-card (inference marker badge in muted green accent, evidence entries as small monospace rows, confidence badge matching existing color tokens), .shadow-profile-card-list grid, and .shadow-profile section wrapper. Use design tokens from src/styles/tokens.css. Include reduced-motion support.
4. Import the new CSS file in src/styles/global.css alongside existing module stylesheets.

5. Create the module renderer at src/modules/shadow-profile/shadow-profile-module.ts — export a renderShadowProfileModule() function that: (a) calls collectSnapshot(), (b) optionally calls checkPermissions(), (c) passes both to applyInferenceRules(), (d) maps resulting InferenceStatement[] to shadow profile cards via createShadowProfileCard, (e) wraps in a section with h2 heading 'Shadow Profile', (f) handles empty-state gracefully (show message if no inferences generated), (g) returns HTMLElement.
6. Update the barrel export at src/modules/shadow-profile/index.ts to re-export renderShadowProfileModule alongside existing inference-schema and inference-rules exports.
7. Mount the module in src/app/main.ts — call renderShadowProfileModule() and append the result to the #dashboard container, positioned after existing modules (OSINT + Permission Debt).

8. Write unit tests in tests/us-026/ covering: (a) shadow-profile-card.test.ts — card renders all required elements (inference marker, statement, evidence entries, confidence badge), marker text is exactly '[Inference]', evidence shows signal/value/source, confidence class is applied correctly, card has semantic structure; (b) shadow-profile-module.test.ts — module returns an HTMLElement, renders cards for each inference, handles zero-inference case, every card contains visible [Inference] marker, no certainty language in rendered text.
9. Run npm run lint, npm run typecheck, and npm run test to validate all checks pass.
testing_recommendations: Unit test: each shadow profile card renders the [Inference] marker as visible text content (not just a class or attribute)
Unit test: evidence entries display signal name, raw value, and API source for each piece of evidence
Unit test: confidence badge renders with correct label ('low', 'medium', 'high') and corresponding CSS class
Unit test: module handles empty inference array gracefully (no errors, renders empty-state message)
Unit test: no certainty language (definitely, certainly, always, never, proves) appears in any rendered card text
Unit test: card element structure uses semantic HTML (article element, heading elements)
Integration test: renderShadowProfileModule returns a valid HTMLElement containing the expected number of inference cards for a given snapshot
CSS validation: shadow-profile.css file exists and contains reduced-motion media query
Run full suite: npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test before marking complete
risks: The inference engine (US-025) may produce zero inferences for certain browser environments — the module must handle the empty state gracefully with an informative message rather than rendering nothing

Reusing vs. creating new card components: creating a new shadow-profile-card (rather than reusing osint-card) adds a small amount of code but is necessary because InferenceStatement has a different shape (evidence array + marker) than OsintCardData — forcing it into the OSINT card would require awkward adapters

The [Inference] marker must be visually prominent and not just metadata — acceptance criteria require every card to be 'visibly marked as inference', so the marker needs deliberate styling as a badge/tag element
Permissions check is async and optional — the module must work correctly both when permissions data is available and when it is not (e.g., if the Permissions API is unsupported)
